body:
status=open
reason=retryable Host-level infrastructure blocker: the machine running this execution worktree is out of disk space, which breaks the Bash tool entirely (not scoped to this worktree). Every Bash invocation, including a trivial `echo hi`, fails before the command even runs: ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-d5b2aa45-20260708T201704-6ee04ae0' This is the harness's own scratch-directory bookkeeping under /tmp, not anything related to the target repo's disk usage. Confirmed the failure is host-wide (not just my process) by spawning a fresh subagent and asking it to run `echo test-ok`; it hit the exact same ENOSPC error creating its own scratch dir. Impact on this bead: - Cannot run `cd cli && go build`/`go test` to verify new test code compiles or passes. - Cannot run `git add`/`git commit` to record any change. - Cannot run `lefthook run pre-commit`. - Read/Edit/Write tools against the actual project filesystem still work (verified by reading cli/internal/agent/tracker_sync.go, tracker_sync_test.go, and part of execute_bead_loop.go, and by writing this file), so this is specific to the Bash tool's /tmp-based scratch directory, not the repo's disk. No source or test changes were made because I cannot verify correctness (compile + test-green) or commit them under these conditions — committing unverified test code would violate the "never commit red code" requirement. Retry expectation: once the host frees space under /tmp (or this bead is redispatched to a worker on a machine with available disk), a retry of this exact bead should proceed normally — the blocker is host disk space, not the bead's scope or acceptance criteria.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes
rationale: status: open
reason: retryable

Host-level infrastructure blocker: the machine running this execution worktree is
out of disk space, which breaks the Bash tool entirely (not scoped to this
worktree). Every Bash invocation, including a trivial `echo hi`, fails before
the command even runs:

    ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-d5b2aa45-20260708T201704-6ee04ae0'

This is the harness's own scratch-directory bookkeeping under /tmp, not
anything related to the target repo's disk usage. Confirmed the failure is
host-wide (not just my process) by spawning a fresh subagent and asking it to
run `echo test-ok`; it hit the exact same ENOSPC error creating its own
scratch dir.

Impact on this bead:
  - Cannot run `cd cli && go build`/`go test` to verify new test code compiles
    or passes.
  - Cannot run `git add`/`git commit` to record any change.
  - Cannot run `lefthook run pre-commit`.
  - Read/Edit/Write tools against the actual project filesystem still work
    (verified by reading cli/internal/agent/tracker_sync.go,
    tracker_sync_test.go, and part of execute_bead_loop.go, and by writing
    this file), so this is specific to the Bash tool's /tmp-based scratch
    directory, not the repo's disk.

No source or test changes were made because I cannot verify correctness
(compile + test-green) or commit them under these conditions — committing
unverified test code would violate the "never commit red code" requirement.

Retry expectation: once the host frees space under /tmp (or this bead is
redispatched to a worker on a machine with available disk), a retry of this
exact bead should proceed normally — the blocker is host disk space, not the
bead's scope or acceptance criteria.
